title: NOC 72405 – Machine fitters | GTR Immigration
description: In 2026, Machine Fitters (NOC 72405) are the precision assembly experts driving Canada’s heavy industrial manufacturing. They are the professionals who take prefabricated metal parts and subassemblies and transform them into massive, functional machines—ranging from aircraft engines and locomotives to agricultural harvesters and food-processing units. Operating at the TEER 2 level, this role has become increasingly technical in 2026, requiring the use of laser-guided alignment tools and digital torque masters to meet the rigorous quality standards of the global supply chain.

classification.teer: FeatureDetailsBroad Occupational Category7 – Trades, transport and equipment operators and related occupationsTEER Category2 (Occupations usually requiring college, apprenticeship, or supervisory experience)Major Group72 – Technical trades and transportation officers and controllersSub-Major Group724 – Machine repair and maintenance trades

duties: Technical Interpretation: Reading and interpreting blueprints, sketches, and diagrams to determine complex assembly sequences.
Precision Assembly: Fitting and assembling prefabricated metal parts to build heavy industrial machinery (e.g., construction, farming, and processing machinery).
Component Installation: Installing major components such as gears, pumps, motors, and sophisticated hydraulic or pneumatic assemblies.
Logistics & Alignment: Moving and aligning subassemblies and components using lifting and transporting devices such as overhead cranes.
Quality Inspection: Inspecting parts, subassemblies, and finished products using digital measuring tools to ensure 2026-grade quality compliance.
employment_requirements: Education: Completion of secondary school is typically required.
Apprenticeship: Completion of a mechanical apprenticeship program (e.g., millwrighting, machining, or heavy equipment repair) OR a four-year machine fitter apprenticeship program is usually required.
Alternative Route: Completion of five years of on-the-job training in a related manufacturing environment.
Specialization: Aircraft engine fitters usually require a two- to three-year college program in aircraft manufacturing or general fabrication.
labour_market_demand: The 2026 outlook is Good to Moderate, with specialized manufacturing hubs actively recruiting for high-precision assembly roles:

Alberta: Good prospects. Driven by the transportation equipment manufacturing and construction sectors. Alberta offers a strong median wage of $37.00/hr, with roughly 41% of the workforce holding a trade certificate.

Ontario: Moderate/Targeted. In February and March 2026, Ontario issued targeted OINP invitations for NOC 72405 under the Employer Job Offer: Foreign Worker stream. Minimum scores for these trades draws started as low as 50, specifically targeting regional manufacturing hubs in Southwestern and Northern Ontario.

Quebec: Strong Demand. Focused in the Chaudière-Appalaches and Montreal regions. The median wage in Quebec is highly competitive at $36.74/hr, with roughly 80% of workers in the aerospace and machinery sectors.

British Columbia: Steady demand. Opportunities are concentrated in the "Mainland/Southwest" region, supporting marine and aerospace fabrication. BC projects stable growth with a median wage of $41.79/hr.
Why Work in Canada as a Machine Fitter?
Lead Industrial Assembly: Gain experience with 2026-grade technologies, including robotic-assisted lifting and Smart Factory (Industry 4.0) assembly protocols.
Diverse Sector Impact: Work across essential industries: machinery manufacturing (49%), transportation equipment (45%), and professional scientific services.
High-Tier Compensation: As a TEER 2 professional, your earning potential is significant, with a national median wage of $35.39/hr and top earners in Quebec reaching over $81,000 annually.
Priority for PR: In early 2026, this NOC has been a consistent highlight in Ontario OINP draws, offering one of the most reliable routes to Permanent Residency for certified assembly experts.
pathways: Ontario Immigrant Nominee Program (OINP): On February 18 and March 25, 2026, OINP issued invitations to candidates in NOC 72405 with a score of 50+ under the Foreign Worker stream. This is a high-success route for those with an Ontario job offer.
2026 Express Entry (Trade Occupations Category): Machine fitters with 12 months of full-time experience (updated 2026 requirement) are eligible for specialized trades draws with lower CRS thresholds.
2026 TR to PR Pathway: A confirmed initiative targeting up to 33,000 temporary residents. Machine fitters in the manufacturing sector are classified as "High-Priority Essential Industrial Workers."
BC PNP Skilled Worker: A reliable route for fitters with a job offer from a BC-based aerospace or machinery manufacturing firm, particularly in the Mainland/Southwest region.
